what_it_does: Specifies a design-only change for DNA.renderUnit that adds warm/plain unit variants, a bottom action contract, CSS background selector changes, frost-card placement, adapter fields, and visual acceptance checks.
observations: Frames four brief asks as one variant-driven rule: warm narrative units use a radial ground to dissolve short-unit voids, while plain reference units keep dense in-flow content.
Documents the current render order for DNA.renderUnit and cites observed, inferred, and verified evidence with file and line references.
Identifies a dead-class defect where closebg is emitted on a .screen root but the gradient rules are scoped to .phone.
Defines u.variant as a normalized data field with default plain, explicitly avoiding DOM measurement inside renderUnit.
Defines u.action as an optional primary action object and maps corpus units to an Open the source action.
Specifies a warm branch composed of screen warmbg/closebg, sbar onimg, existing header, p-frost words card, p-act, p-rail, and p-hatch.
Calls for unscoping warmbg and closebg to bare classes and adding .screen .p-frost margin using existing tokens.
Includes a verify-by-sight checklist for warm short units, plain dense units, plain short units, phone mockups, and one-action-per-screen behavior.
standouts: Treats visual emptiness as a substrate/background problem rather than a vertical-centering problem.
Separates renderer purity from variant selection by placing warm/plain choice in adapters.
Uses existing CSS classes and design tokens as a constraint throughout the proposed implementation.
Keeps warm narrative furniture sparse and plain reference furniture dense.
conventions: Uses sectioned Markdown with numbered subsections, tables, inline code identifiers, and explicit slot lists.
Labels claims as Observed, Inferred, or Verified and ties many claims to file-line citations.
States implementation constraints before proposed edits, including no bespoke px and no shared-file edits by the author.
Distinguishes data contracts, renderer slots, CSS changes, adapter changes, and visual acceptance criteria.
concerns: Several story action and archetype mappings are explicitly marked as inferred or not yet verified inside the file.
The spec contains exact implementation expressions while also describing itself as design-only, which creates a tight coupling between design intent and code shape.
notes: A design spec for adding an interpretive warm/plain layer to unit rendering without measuring layout.
questions: Which story archetypes actually exist in content/stories/*.json?
Should warm story units always receive a rail when no navigation target exists?
What caller field should control optional plain countstrip or rail rendering?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the content: it proposes design and implementation guidance for DNA to build and verify a warmer short-unit rendering path plus bottom actions.
apparent_themes: separation of narrative and reference substrates
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to translate visual proof findings into a constrained render/CSS plan that makes short narrative units feel intentional while leaving dense reference units structurally familiar.
novelty: It treats a warm background class as an interpretive rendering substrate and makes variant selection a normalized data concern rather than a layout measurement.
contribution: Contributes a neutral warm/plain substrate model, data fields for variant and action, constrained CSS adjustments, and a sight-based acceptance checklist that a fused system could retain as rendering guidance.
summary_for_embedding: Design-only spec for DNA.renderUnit proposing warm/plain variants, unscoped warm backgrounds, p-frost placement, bottom p-act actions, adapter data contracts, and verify-by-sight criteria.
intention_for_embedding: Created to guide implementation of a short-unit visual fix and bottom action pattern while preserving renderer purity and existing class/token usage.
